question:
[PICTURE REQUIRED]
In pentagon ABCDE shown above, each side is 1 centimeter. If a particle starts at point A and travels clockwise 723 centimeters along ABCDE, at which point will the particle stop?

solution.strategy: Perimeter = 5 cm. 723 ÷ 5 = 144 remainder 3. So 144 full laps plus 3 cm: A→B→C→D. Stop at D.
steps: After 3 cm from A: at D

question:
[PICTURE REQUIRED]
In pentagon ABCDE shown above, each side is 1 centimeter. If a particle starts at point A and travels clockwise 523 centimeters along ABCDE, at which point will the particle stop?

[PICTURE REQUIRED]
In pentagon ABCDE shown above, each side is 1 centimeter. If a particle starts at point A and travels clockwise 18 centimeters along ABCDE, at which point will the particle stop?

[PICTURE REQUIRED]
In pentagon ABCDE shown above, each side is 1 centimeter. If a particle starts at point A and travels clockwise 7 centimeters along ABCDE, at which point will the particle stop?

solution.strategy: 7 ÷ 5 = 1 R 2. After 2 cm from A: at C.

question:
[PICTURE REQUIRED]
In pentagon ABCDE shown above, each side is 1 centimeter. If a particle starts at point A and travels clockwise 11 centimeters along ABCDE, at which point will the particle stop?

solution.strategy: 11 ÷ 5 = 2 R 1. After 1 cm from A: at B.
